author: y4rr
content:
Plan:
Layer 1 — Surface inventory TOML at ferrotorch-train/tests/conformance/_surface_inventory.toml. Will enumerate all pub items from the 8 source modules (amp, callback, checkpoint, grad_utils, history, learner, metric, tensorboard). This is the denominator for coverage.

Layer 2 — Reference fixtures at ferrotorch-train/tests/conformance/fixtures.json. Script: scripts/regenerate_train_fixtures.py using torch==2.11.0. Fixtures cover training-loop semantics: gradient accumulation over multiple batches, checkpoint serialization round-trip, deterministic seed for reproducibility, grad clipping (L2 norm and value), EMA decay update, metric accumulation. These use minimum-viable workloads — no actual GPU required, purely CPU with tiny tensors to make fixture values reproducible.

Layer 3 — Conformance tests at ferrotorch-train/tests/conformance_train.rs. Will split by module for clarity (one #[test] per semantic scenario, loading fixtures from fixtures.json). Test categories: metric semantics, grad_utils parity, amp context, checkpoint sequential, history statistics, EarlyStopping trigger, EMA decay arithmetic, TensorBoard protobuf framing.

Layer 4 — Strict surface-coverage gate at ferrotorch-train/tests/conformance_surface_coverage.rs. Same design as ferrotorch-core gate: reads _surface_inventory.toml, reads _surface_exclusions.toml (for items that are legitimately untestable), scans conformance_*.rs sources, fails if any item is uncovered without an exclusion entry with a tracking_issue field.

Reference: torch==2.11.0 (matching existing fixture pin across the workspace).

Constraints:
- No bug fixing — cascade skip + file crosslink issue
- No workspace dep changes
- No cargo publish / git push / git tag
- ferrotorch-train/Cargo.toml currently has empty [dev-dependencies] — serde_json and serde needed for fixture loading; toml needed for TOML parsing
